messages.content: Hello
Hi! How can I assist you today?

How to better say: Hello, the Crypto Futures terminal is currently available in the web browser edition and via the Android app. iOS app support is currently in development and will be added in the nearest updates as we progress

Greetings! The Crypto Futures terminal can currently be accessed through the web browser edition and the Android app. We are currently working on developing support for the iOS app, which will be added in the upcoming updates as we make progress.

How to better say: It can take time for the compliance office to review all the necessary details regarding the account. As a rule the deadline will be extended, your funds are safe once the case has been reviewed and you will be able to withdraw

The compliance office needs sufficient time to thoroughly review all the necessary account details. It is common for deadlines to be extended in such cases. Rest assured that your funds are secure during the review process, and once the case has been reviewed, you will be able to make withdrawals.

How to better say: Crypto Futures currently utilizes position netting, i.e. when executing additional positions with the same instrument, they are added or reduced to/from the initial position. In this case you can place an opposing order for a specified price point which will reduce part of the position once executed

At Crypto Futures, we currently employ position netting, which means that when executing additional positions with the same instrument, they are either added to or reduced from the initial position. In this scenario, you have the option to place an opposing order at a specific price point, which will reduce a portion of the position once it is executed.

i see, so lets say im in 0.75 btc short right now from 26025 and at around 25850 i want to close 1/3rd of my position. does that mean i need to create a buy order at 25850 worth 0.25 btc? please do correct me if im wrong, this is a new way to TP for me

Yes, you are correct! If you are currently in a short position of 0.75 BTC at a price of 26025 and you wish to close 1/3rd of your position at around 25850, you would need to create a buy order for 0.25 BTC at the price of 25850. This will effectively reduce your position size by 1/3rd once the order is executed. This approach allows you to take profit or partially close your position at specific price levels.
sweet thank you for the clarification! thats all the questions i had, have a good rest of your day/night
You're welcome! I'm glad I could help. If you have any more questions in the future, feel free to ask. Have a wonderful day or night too!
Do you like cats or dogs better joke

As an AI, I don't have personal preferences or emotions, so I don't have a preference between cats and dogs. However, I'm always happy to hear jokes about them! Do you have a cat or dog joke you'd like to share?
Do you like cats or dogs better? can you make a joke based on that sentence

Sure! Here's a lighthearted joke:

Why did the AI prefer cats over dogs?
Because cats have a "pawsome" ability to keep secrets, and the AI loves a good mystery!
How to better say: Client suggested adding a ‘save chart’ button in the trading terminal which would carry over current chart drawing when accessing the platform across different setups
The client has proposed the inclusion of a 'save chart' button in the trading terminal, which would retain the current chart drawings when accessing the platform on different setups.

Hi there! How to reply to my shift manager who said this: Hey Nick. In September, in connection with the release of the last trainee to the line, it became possible to reduce the number of night shifts you have. Starting September 22, your chart will follow the pattern
day-night, day-night, night-night
I remember that you prefer a shift schedule with repetitive rotation of day-night shifts. Such a schedule, tentatively, I will be able to deliver to you starting in November. Interns are just starting to go into 2/2 shifts, night shifts are in charge, and until we're confident in their skills, day shifts will heavily predominate. I'll post the schedule soon.
Also, from September, the incentive program for going to night shifts after the 5th at a double rate is curtailed. It was a temporary program and now, when the trainees go into line, it should relieve all team members in general on work.







Anatoly
   10:02AM
For August, payments at a double rate will, of course, be made, you will receive along with salary

Hi Anatoly,

Thank you for the update. I appreciate that the number of night shifts will be reduced starting from September 22nd, following the pattern of day-night, day-night, night-night. I look forward to the possibility of having a schedule with a repetitive rotation of day-night shifts starting in November.

I understand that the interns are transitioning into 2/2 shifts, and it's important to ensure their skills are developed before incorporating more night shifts. Please share the schedule once it is finalized.

Regarding the incentive program for night shifts, I understand that it was a temporary program and will be curtailed now that the trainees are joining the line. I anticipate receiving the August payments at a double rate along with my salary.

Thank you for keeping me informed.

Best regards,
Nick
How to make it sound that I still appreciate the temporary pay rise but I'm just to work in any case and also to make it a little bit more informal

Hey Anatoly,

Thanks for the update, buddy! I totally get it that starting September 22nd, we'll be seeing less night shifts in the schedule. It'll be day-night, day-night, night-night from now on. Can't wait for November when you'll hook me up with that sweet repetitive rotation of day-night shifts I love so much. It'll be worth the wait, my man!

I understand that the newbies need some time to get their groove on, so day shifts will be dominating for a while. No worries, I'm up for any challenge and happy to help out the team.

By the way, I want to give a shoutout for the temporary double rate pay program for night shifts in August. I really appreciated that extra boost and looking forward to receiving it along with my regular salary.

Keep me posted with the schedule, dude!

Cheers,
Nick
